title: CAA Gathers Top Filmmakers, Studio Execs for Cannes Symposium on the Future of Film With Finch & Partners
content:
ByMatt Donnelly

Chief Correspondent

Saturday mornings at the Cannes Film Festival are usually reserved for oceanside brunch, shopping the Rue d’Antibes or (for some Hollywood types) finally getting home from Friday night’s parties.

CAAbroke the pattern at this year’s fest with a pedigreed summit on a dizzying list of challenges currently facing the movie business – particularly the art house fare that Cannes loves to reward, the type that CAA is instrumental in packaging and selling to global buyers.

Dubbed “The Future of … Film Symposium”, the mega-agency welcomed talent like Darren Aronofsky, James Gray (“Paper Tiger”), Nadine Labaki (“Capernaum”) Whit Stillman (“Love and Friendship”) and Juan Antionio Bayona (“Society of the Snow”) for a series of conversations – covering theatrical windows, artificial intelligence, audience attention spans and emerging models to finance film.

CAA co-hosted the event with Finch & Partners in Antibes, at the stunning Villa Dorane (owned by art collector and photographer Jean Pigozzi). Pressure was low at the off-record event, meaning talent and top execs could share unfiltered conversations about the obstacles they face in the present moment.

CAA Media Finance boss Roeg Sutherland recruited an impressive list of leaders from the corporate and indie spaces including: Efe Cakarel of Mubi; Noah Sacco of A24; Michael Barker of Sony Pictures Classics; Ryan Werner and Jeff Deutschmann of Neon; Peter Kujawski of Focus Features; Sanford Panitch of Columbia Pictures; Justin Wilkes of Imagine Documentaries; Ben Kramer of Black Bear; Sundance Film Festival head Eugene Hernandez; Netflix awards guru Lisa Taback; and CAA’s Megan Crawford and Sarah Schweitzman.

Expansive views of the French Riviera accompanied thoughtful panels – including some interesting (and alarming?) data from Crawford on how sports gambling is beginning to significantly distract young men from the movies. Lunch was served in a stunning grotto, as guests mingled in Villa Dorane’s private gardens and ogled sculptures and installations from Pigozzi’s collection.

CAA intends to replicate the event at cultural gatherings moving forward, expanding the purview to mediums other than film.
title_zh: CAA（創意藝術代理公司）聚集頂尖電影製作人及影業高層，舉辦與芬奇與夥伴（Finch & Partners）合作的坎城電影節研討會，探討電影的未來。
content_zh:
由馬特·多納利(Matt Donnelly)報導

首席記者

在坎城電影節的星期六早晨，通常是留給海邊早午餐、在安提布街(Rue d’Antibes)購物，或者（對於一些好萊塢人士來說）終於從星期五晚上的派對回家。

今年的電影節，CAA打破了這一慣例，舉辦了一場具有深厚背景的峰會，討論當前電影業面臨的一系列挑戰，特別是坎城喜愛獎勵的藝術電影類型，而CAA在包裝和銷售這些電影給全球買家方面發揮了重要作用。

這場名為「電影的未來研討會(The Future of ... Film Symposium)」的盛會，邀請了達倫·阿倫諾夫斯基(Darren Aronofsky)、詹姆斯·格雷(James Gray)（《紙老虎(Paper Tiger)》）、納丁·拉巴基(Nadine Labaki)（《卡比納(Capernaum)》）、惠特·史提爾曼(Whit Stillman)（《愛與友誼(Love and Friendship)》）以及胡安·安東尼奧·巴約納(Juan Antonio Bayona)（《雪之社(Society of the Snow)》）等人才進行了一系列對話，涵蓋了戲院放映窗口、人工智慧、觀眾注意力持續時間以及新興的電影融資模式。

CAA與芬奇合夥公司(Finch & Partners)共同主辦了此次活動，地點位於壯觀的多蘭別墅(Villa Dorane)，該別墅由藝術收藏家及攝影師讓·皮戈齊(Jean Pigozzi)擁有。此次非公開活動的壓力較小，這意味著人才和高層主管可以分享他們在當前時刻面臨的障礙的真實對話。

CAA媒體財務負責人羅格·薩瑟蘭(Roeg Sutherland)招募了一份令人印象深刻的企業和獨立領域領導者名單，包括：Mubi的埃菲·卡卡雷(Efe Cakarel)；A24的諾亞·薩科(Noah Sacco)；索尼經典影業的邁克爾·巴克(Michael Barker)；Neon的瑞安·維爾納(Ryan Werner)和傑夫·德意士曼(Jeff Deutschmann)；Focus Features的彼得·庫亞夫斯基(Peter Kujawski)；哥倫比亞影業的桑福德·潘尼奇(Sanford Panitch)；Imagine Documentaries的賈斯廷·威爾克斯(Justin Wilkes)；黑熊影業的本·克雷默(Ben Kramer)；聖丹斯電影節的負責人尤金·埃爾南德斯(Eugene Hernandez)；Netflix獎項專家莉莎·塔巴克(Lisa Taback)；以及CAA的梅根·克勞福德(Megan Crawford)和莎拉·施維茨曼(Sarah Schweitzman)。

法國里維埃拉的壯麗景色伴隨著深思熟慮的座談會，其中包括克勞福德提供的一些有趣（甚至令人擔憂？）的數據，顯示體育博彩開始顯著分散年輕男性對電影的注意力。午餐在一個壯觀的洞穴中供應，賓客們在多蘭別墅的私人花園中交流，並欣賞皮戈齊的收藏品中的雕塑和裝置藝術。

CAA計劃在未來的文化聚會中複製這一活動，將範疇擴展到電影以外的媒介。
summary_zh:
由馬特·多納利(Matt Donnelly)報導

首席記者

在坎城電影節的星期六早晨，通常是留給海邊早午餐、在安提布街(Rue d’Antibes)購物，或者（對於一些好萊塢人士來說）終於從星期五晚上的派對回家。
